draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers works better with conversational, approachable tone over corporate stiffness
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Casual and friendly
facade.acceptImplies: Users prefer warm, conversational language and playful micro-interactions that feel like working with a friend, not an enterprise tool
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer formal, polished, professional language that signals reliability and seriousness — like traditional business software

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer low-light interfaces for late-night client work and invoice tracking
facade.label: Dark mode
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark-first design, muted accent colors, high contrast text for CRM dashboards
facade.rejectImplies: Light or warm backgrounds may be preferred; test bright, airy aesthetic instead

evidence.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers works better with conversational, approachable tone over corporate stiffness
evidence.implication: Users prefer warm, conversational language and playful micro-interactions that feel like working with a friend, not an enterprise tool

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a persistent left navigation for quick project access, paired with card-based layouts for client details
facade.axisTargeted: navigation persistence vs. tab-based switching; information density (cards vs. lists)
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values always-visible navigation and scannable card layouts for managing multiple client relationships
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers tab-based or full-width layouts; may find sidebars cluttered or cards too sparse for CRM data density

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer scannable contact cards over dense data tables for quick client lookup
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs. key metrics — visual density and cognitive load in client views
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick visual scanning, prefers whitespace, likely wants card-based layouts throughout CRM for contacts, projects, invoices
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer compact table views, density over spacing, or wants customizable column-based layouts for power-user workflows

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer fluid, animated interactions (like drawer opens) over instant state changes—it feels more responsive and less jarring when managing multiple client contacts.
facade.axisTargeted: Motion intensity: fluid animations vs instant interactions
facade.label: Smooth slide open
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward transitions that feel polished and deliberate. Suggests they value perceived responsiveness and visual feedback in CRM workflows (contact opens, panels expand, etc.).
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers snappy, instant interactions—no delay, no animation overhead. Suggests they want speed and directness; motion feels like friction to their workflow.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to interface warmth (cream, soft shadows, rounded corners) than stark minimalism for a tool managing their livelihood
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into cream/beige/soft gold palettes, rounded UI elements, and soft diffused lighting effects in the interface. Suggests freelancers want their business tool to feel inviting and human-centered rather than corporate or clinical.
facade.rejectImplies: Explore cooler, cleaner palettes (bright whites, cool grays, blues). Suggests freelancers prefer crisp, efficient, no-nonsense visual language—something that signals professionalism and clarity over comfort.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants all tools visible at once vs. hidden behind menus/modals—reveals preference for dashboard density and navigation style in a tool-heavy CRM
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator voice
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals mood preference for daily work in a CRM they'll use constantly
Whether user prefers data-dense tables/lists vs. card-based summaries with key info highlighted—reveals how they want client and project information presented
Whether user expects instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals preference for snappy responsiveness vs. polished, guided interactions in a CRM workflow

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants all tools visible at once vs. hidden behind menus/modals—reveals preference for dashboard density and navigation style in a tool-heavy CRM
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals mood preference for daily work in a CRM they'll use constantly
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-dense tables/lists vs. card-based summaries with key info highlighted—reveals how they want client and project information presented
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user expects instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals preference for snappy responsiveness vs. polished, guided interactions in a CRM workflow
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers respond better to delightful, animated feedback (button states, loading states, success confirmations) that feel rewarding rather than sterile, minimal interactions
facade.axisTargeted: Voice and Personality — playful vs serious
facade.label: Playful micro-interactions
facade.acceptImplies: The CRM should include animated confirmations, fun loading states, celebratory moments (e.g., confetti on invoice sent), and interactive feedback that makes routine tasks feel rewarding and less tedious
facade.rejectImplies: Freelancers prefer understated, no-nonsense interactions—clean state changes without animation or humor—keeping the tool businesslike and focused on efficiency over delight

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants all tools visible at once vs. hidden behind menus/modals—reveals preference for dashboard density and navigation style in a tool-heavy CRM

Whether user expects instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals preference for snappy responsiveness vs. polished, guided interactions in a CRM workflow
